extra.message: User input from request.POST['user_id'] is directly concatenated into a raw SQL query using string formatting (line 75-76). An attacker can inject SQL commands via the user_id parameter to bypass the ORDER BY clause and manipulate the query logic.

User input from the 'ip' parameter is directly concatenated into a raw SQL query using string formatting. An attacker can inject SQL commands via the ip parameter to manipulate the query and access unauthorized data.

User-controlled 'path' parameter from request.POST is directly used in HttpResponseRedirect() at line 43 without validation. An attacker can provide a malicious URL to redirect users to external websites.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: User input from request.GET['name'] is directly concatenated with BASE_DIR to construct a file path without validation. An attacker can use path traversal sequences (../) to access arbitrary files on the system.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: User input from request.POST['user'] is passed to pickle.loads() without validation. Pickle deserialization of untrusted data can lead to arbitrary code execution.
User input from request.GET['class'] is passed directly to eval() without validation. This allows arbitrary Python code execution by an attacker who can control the 'class' parameter.

cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code

extra.message: User-controlled filename from uploaded_file.name is used in os.system() command without escaping. An attacker can craft a malicious filename with shell metacharacters to execute arbitrary OS commands.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: Django SECRET_KEY is hardcoded in the settings.py file. This secret should never be committed to version control. An attacker with access to this secret can forge session tokens and authentication cookies.
